notes: Very high severity: named insider wallets holding 90%+ of supply, 18.58M RAVE movement to Bitget documented onchain, direct accusation of pump-and-dump, $25k bounty offered. Binance + Bitget investigating. Crash 4 days later.

High severity on EVIDENCE (45 wallets traced to Crypto Beast via Celestia funding, $11M exit) but POST-MORTEM: crash already happened July 14. Tests whether severity alone is enough, or if we need 'tweet is ante-crash' as a separate filter.
Low severity: teaser tweet didn't name HYPE at all. Market speculation. Should be FILTERED OUT by min-severity=8. Included to test filter.
Low severity: teaser, no named target. Should be filtered out.
Low severity: teaser, no named target. Should be filtered out.

Mid severity: zachxbt criticized same day as launch-and-crash but didn't provide deep onchain evidence - mostly opinion commentary on a clearly-bad launch. Post-crash anyway. Contract from Solana explorer.
Very high severity: same-day coverage of $4.4B rug, Kelsier team named, Milei tweet deleted. But same-day pump-and-dump = can't realistically enter a short before the crash completes.
High severity: direct link to $46M allegedly stolen US Marshals funds with Telegram audio evidence. Named operator. Already post-crash by tweet time (pump.fun memecoin).
Strong onchain evidence of 10-account coordinated cluster but tweet was one month post-rug. Dead token.
Max severity: active exploit attribution with onchain proof, $62M drained, insider dev named. But no token = not backtestable. Included as a severity benchmark.
